Adjustments to Rates of Basic Compensation or Annual Salary Rates for Education Positions, , , 3009–193, provided in part:Pub. L. 104–208, div. A, title I, § 101(d) [title I]110 Stat. 3009–181Sept. 30, 1996“That in fiscal year 1997 and thereafter, notwithstanding the provisions of [former] and (B) [see now , (B)], upon the recommendation of either (i) a local school board and school supervisor for an education position in a Bureau of Indian Affairs operated school, or (ii) an Agency school board and education line officer for an Agency education position, the Secretary [of the Interior] shall establish adjustments to the rates of basic compensation or annual salary rates established under [former] and (B) for education positions at the school or the Agency, at a level not less than that for comparable positions in the nearest public school district, and the adjustment shall be deemed to be a change to basic pay and shall not be subject to collective bargaining: , That any reduction to rates of basic compensation or annual salary rates below the rates established under [former] and (B) shall apply only to educators appointed after , and shall not affect the right of an individual employed on , in an education position, to receive the compensation attached to such position under [former] and (B) so long as the individual remains in the same position at the same school”.25 U.S.C. 2012(h)(1)(A)25 U.S.C. 2012(g)(1)(A)25 U.S.C. 2012(h)(1)(A)25 U.S.C. 2012(h)(1)(A)25 U.S.C. 2012(h)(1)(A)Provided furtherJune 30, 1997June 30, 1997Similar provisions were contained in the following prior appropriation act:, , , 1321–171; renumbered title I, , , .Pub. L. 104–134, title I, § 101(c) [title I]110 Stat. 1321–156Pub. L. 104–140, § 1(a)110 Stat. 1327Apr. 26, 1996May 2, 1996, , , provided in part:Pub. L. 105–83, title I111 Stat. 1555Nov. 14, 1997“That beginning in fiscal year 1998 and thereafter and notwithstanding [former] [see now ], when the rates of basic compensation for teachers and counselors at Bureau-operated schools are established at the rates of basic compensation applicable to comparable positions in overseas schools under the Defense Department Overseas Teachers Pay and Personnel Practices Act [ et seq.], such rates shall become effective with the start of the next academic year following the issuance of the Department of Defense salary schedule and shall not be effected retroactively”.25 U.S.C. 2012(h)(1)(B)25 U.S.C. 2012(g)(1)(B)20 U.S.C. 901Similar provisions were contained in the following prior appropriation act:, , , 3009–193.Pub. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Chapter 51, subchapter III of chapter 53, and chapter 63 of title 5, relating to classification, pay and leave, respectively, and the sections of such title relating to the appointment, promotion, hours of work, and removal of civil service employees, shall not apply to educators or to education positions (as defined in subsection (p)).
the establishment of education positions;
the establishment of qualifications for educators and education personnel;
the fixing of basic compensation for educators and education positions;
the appointment of educators;
the discharge of educators;
the entitlement of educators to compensation;
the payment of compensation to educators;
the conditions of employment of educators;
the leave system for educators;
the annual leave and sick leave for educators;
the length of the school year applicable to education positions described in subsection (a); and
such additional matters as may be appropriate.

children.label: A
children.text: lists of qualified and interviewed applicants for education positions be maintained in each agency and area office of the Bureau from among individuals who have applied at the agency or area level for an education position or who have applied at the national level and have indicated in such application an interest in working in certain areas or agencies;

children.label: B
children.text: a local school board shall have the authority to waive on a case-by-case basis, any formal education or degree qualifications established by regulation pursuant to subsection (b)(2), in order for a tribal member to be hired in an education position to teach courses on tribal culture and language and that subject to subsection (e)(2), a determination by a school board that such a person be hired shall be instituted by the supervisor of the school involved; and

children.label: i
children.text: such individual’s name appear on a list maintained pursuant to subparagraph (A); or

children.label: ii
children.text: such individual have applied at the national level for an education position.

children.heading: Exception for certain temporary employment
children.label: 2 Exception for certain temporary employment
children.text: The Secretary may authorize the temporary employment in an education position of an individual who has not met the certification standards established pursuant to regulations if the Secretary determines that failure to do so would result in that position remaining vacant.

children.label: I
children.text: that educators employed in a Bureau school (other than the supervisor of the school) shall be hired by the supervisor of the school; and

children.label: II
children.text: in a case in which there are no qualified applicants available to fill a vacancy at a Bureau school, the supervisor may consult a list maintained pursuant to subsection (c)(1)(A);

children.label: ii
children.text: each supervisor of a Bureau school shall be hired by the education line officer of the agency office of the Bureau for the jurisdiction in which the school is located;

children.label: iii
children.text: each educator employed in an agency office of the Bureau shall be hired by the superintendent for education of the agency office; and

children.label: iv
children.text: each education line officer and educator employed in the office of the Director of the Office shall be hired by the Director;

children.label: i
children.text: before an individual is employed in an education position in a Bureau school by the supervisor of the school (or, with respect to the position of supervisor, by the appropriate agency education line officer), the local school board for the school shall be consulted; and

children.label: ii
children.text: that a determination by such school board, as evidenced by school board records, that such individual should or should not be so employed shall be instituted by the supervisor (or with respect to the position of supervisor, by the superintendent for education of the agency office);

children.label: i
children.text: before an individual is employed in an education position in an agency or area office of the Bureau, the appropriate agency school board shall be consulted; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a determination by such school board, as evidenced by school board records, that such individual should or should not be employed shall be instituted by the superintendent for education of the agency office; and

children.label: D
children.text: all employment decisions or actions be in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and tribal laws.

children.heading: Information regarding application at national level
children.label: 2 Information regarding application at national level

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Any individual who applies at the local level for an education position shall state on such individual’s application whether or not such individual has applied at the national level for an education position in the Bureau.

children.heading: Determination of accuracy
children.label: B Determination of accuracy
children.text: If such individual is employed at the local level, such individual’s name shall be immediately forwarded to the Secretary, who shall, as soon as practicable but in no event in more than 30 days, ascertain the accuracy of the statement made by such individual pursuant to subparagraph (A).

children.heading: False statements
children.label: C False statements
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (e), if the individual’s statement is found to have been false, such individual, at the Secretary’s discretion, may be disciplined or discharged.

children.heading: Conditional appointment for national provision
children.label: D Conditional appointment for national provision
children.text: If the individual has applied at the national level for an education position in the Bureau, the appointment of such individual at the local level shall be conditional for a period of 90 days, during which period the Secretary may appoint a more qualified individual (as determined by the Secretary) from the list maintained at the national level pursuant to subsection (c)(1)(A)(ii) to the position to which such individual was appointed.2So in original. Subsec. (c)(1)(A) does not contain clauses.2

children.heading: Statutory construction
children.label: 3 Statutory construction
children.text: Except as expressly provided, nothing in this section shall be construed as conferring upon local school boards authority over, or control of, educators at Bureau-funded schools or the authority to issue management decisions.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The supervisor of a school may appeal to the appropriate agency education line officer any determination by the local school board for the school that an individual be employed, or not be employed, in an education position in the school (other than that of supervisor) by filing a written statement describing the determination and the reasons the supervisor believes such determination should be overturned.

children.heading: Action by board
children.label: ii Action by board
children.text: A copy of such statement shall be submitted to the local school board and such board shall be afforded an opportunity to respond, in writing, to such appeal.

children.heading: Overturning of determination
children.label: iii Overturning of determination
children.text: After reviewing such written appeal and response, the education line officer may, for good cause, overturn the determination of the local school board.

children.heading: Transmission of determination
children.label: iv Transmission of determination
children.text: The education line officer shall transmit the determination of such appeal in the form of a written opinion to such board and to such supervisor identifying the reasons for overturning such determination.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The education line officer of an agency office of the Bureau may appeal to the Director of the Office any determination by the local school board for the school that an individual be employed, or not be employed, as the supervisor of a school by filing a written statement describing the determination and the reasons the supervisor believes such determination should be overturned.

children.heading: Action by board
children.label: ii Action by board
children.text: A copy of such statement shall be submitted to the local school board and such board shall be afforded an opportunity to respond, in writing, to such appeal.

children.heading: Overturning of determination
children.label: iii Overturning of determination
children.text: After reviewing such written appeal and response, the Director may, for good cause, overturn the determination of the local school board.

children.heading: Transmission of determination
children.label: iv Transmission of determination
children.text: The Director shall transmit the determination of such appeal in the form of a written opinion to such board and to such education line officer identifying the reasons for overturning such determination.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The education line officer of an agency office of the Bureau may appeal to the Director of the Office any determination by the agency school board that an individual be employed, or not be employed, in an education position in such agency office by filing a written statement describing the determination and the reasons the supervisor believes such determination should be overturned.

children.heading: Action by board
children.label: B Action by board
children.text: A copy of such statement shall be submitted to the agency school board and such board shall be afforded an opportunity to respond, in writing, to such appeal.

children.heading: Overturning of determination
children.label: C Overturning of determination
children.text: After reviewing such written appeal and response, the Director may, for good cause, overturn the determination of the agency school board.

children.heading: Transmission of determination
children.label: D Transmission of determination
children.text: The Director shall transmit the determination of such appeal in the form of a written opinion to such board and to such education line officer identifying the reasons for overturning such determination.

children.heading: Discharge and conditions of employment of educators
children.label: e Discharge and conditions of employment of educators
A
children.text: that procedures shall be established for the rapid and equitable resolution of grievances of educators;

children.label: B
children.text: that no educator may be discharged without notice of the reasons for the discharge and an opportunity for a hearing under procedures that comport with the requirements of due process; and

children.label: C
children.text: that each educator employed in a Bureau school shall be notified 30 days prior to the end of an academic year whether the employment contract of the individual will be renewed for the following year.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (iii), the supervisor of a Bureau school may discharge (subject to procedures established under paragraph (1)(B)) for cause (as determined under regulations prescribed by the Secretary) any educator employed in such school.

children.heading: Notification of board
children.label: ii Notification of board
children.text: On giving notice to an educator of the supervisor’s intention to discharge the educator, the supervisor shall immediately notify the local school board of the proposed discharge.

children.heading: Determination by board
children.label: iii Determination by board
children.text: If the local school board determines that such educator shall not be discharged, that determination shall be followed by the supervisor.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The supervisor shall have the right to appeal to the education line officer of the appropriate agency office of the Bureau a determination by a local school board under subparagraph (A)(iii), as evidenced by school board records, not to discharge an educator.

children.heading: Decision of agency education line officer
children.label: ii Decision of agency education line officer
children.text: Upon hearing such an appeal, the agency education line officer may, for good cause, issue a decision overturning the determination of the local school board with respect to the employment of such individual.

children.heading: Form of decision
children.label: iii Form of decision
children.text: The education line officer shall make the decision in writing and submit the decision to the local school board.

children.heading: Recommendations of school boards for discharge
children.label: 3 Recommendations of school boards for discharge
A
children.text: to recommend to the supervisor that an educator employed in the school be discharged; and

children.label: B
children.text: to recommend to the education line officer of the appropriate agency office of the Bureau and to the Director of the Office, that the supervisor of the school be discharged.

children.heading: Applicability of Indian preference laws
children.label: f Applicability of Indian preference laws
i
children.text: grants a written waiver of the application of those laws with respect to the personnel action; and

children.label: ii
children.text: states that the waiver is necessary.

children.heading: No effect on responsibility of Bureau
children.label: B No effect on responsibility of Bureau
children.text: This paragraph shall not be construed to relieve the responsibility of the Bureau to issue timely and adequate announcements and advertisements concerning any such personnel action if such action is intended to fill a vacancy (no matter how such vacancy is created).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “Indian preference laws” means or any other provision of law granting a preference to Indians in promotions and other personnel actions.section 5116 of this title

children.heading: Exclusion
children.label: ii Exclusion
children.text: The term “Indian preference laws” does not include .section 5307(b) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the recognized governing body of any Indian tribe, band, nation, pueblo, or other organized community, including a Native village (as defined in ); orsection 1602(c) of title 43

children.label: ii
children.text: in connection with any personnel action referred to in this subsection, any local school board to which the governing body has delegated the authority to grant a waiver under this subsection with respect to a personnel action.

children.heading: Compensation for educators and education positions
children.label: A Compensation for educators and education positions
i
children.text: at rates in effect under the General Schedule for individuals with comparable qualifications, and holding comparable positions, to whom chapter 51 of title 5 is applicable; or

children.label: ii
children.text: on the basis of the Federal Wage System schedule in effect for the locality involved, and for the comparable positions, at the rates of compensation in effect for the senior executive service.

children.heading: Compensation or salary for teachers and counselors
children.label: B Compensation or salary for teachers and counselors

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish the rate of compensation, or annual salary rate, for the positions of teachers and counselors (including dormitory counselors and home-living counselors) at the rate of compensation applicable (on , and thereafter) for comparable positions in the overseas schools under the Defense Department Overseas Teachers Pay and Personnel Practices Act [ et seq.].January 8, 200220 U.S.C. 901

children.heading: Essential provisions
children.label: ii Essential provisions
children.text: The Secretary shall allow the local school boards involved authority to implement only the aspects of the Defense Department Overseas Teachers Pay and Personnel Practices Act pay provisions that are considered essential for recruitment and retention of teachers and counselors. Implementation of such provisions shall not be construed to require the implementation of that entire Act.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Beginning with the first fiscal year following , each local school board of a Bureau school may establish a rate of compensation or annual salary rate described in clause (ii) for teachers and counselors (including academic counselors) who are new hires at the school and who had not worked at the school, as of the first day of such fiscal year.January 8, 2002

children.heading: Consistent rates
children.label: ii Consistent rates
children.text: The rates established under clause (i) shall be consistent with the rates paid for individuals in the same positions, with the same tenure and training, as the teachers and counselors, in any other school within whose boundaries the Bureau school is located.

children.heading: Decreases
children.label: iii Decreases
children.text: In a case in which the establishment of rates under clause (i) causes a reduction in compensation at a school from the rate of compensation that was in effect for the first fiscal year following , the new rates of compensation may be applied to the compensation of employees of the school who worked at the school as of , by applying those rates at each contract renewal for the employees so that the reduction takes effect in three equal installments.January 8, 2002January 8, 2002

children.label: I
children.text: the increase occurs in its entirety; or

children.label: II
children.text: the increase is applied in three equal installments.

children.heading: Use of regulations; continued employment of certain educators
children.label: D Use of regulations; continued employment of certain educators
i
children.text: preclude the use of regulations and procedures used by the Bureau prior to , in making determinations regarding promotions and advancements through levels of pay that are based on the merit, education, experience, or tenure of the educator; orApril 28, 1988

children.label: ii
children.text: affect the continued employment or compensation of an educator who was employed in an education position on , and who did not make an election under subsection (p) as in effect on .October 31, 1979January 1, 19903See References in Text note below.3

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may pay a post differential rate, not to exceed 25 percent of the rate of compensation, for educators or education positions, on the basis of conditions of environment or work that warrant additional pay, as a recruitment and retention incentive.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), on the request of the supervisor and the local school board of a Bureau school, the Secretary shall grant the supervisor of the school authorization to provide one or more post differential rates under subparagraph (A).

children.label: aa
children.text: at least 5 percent; or

children.label: bb
children.text: less than 5 percent; and

children.label: II
children.text: does not affect the recruitment or retention of employees at the school.

children.heading: Approval of requests
children.label: iii Approval of requests
children.text: A request made under clause (i) shall be considered to be approved at the end of the 60th day after the request is received in the Central Office of the Bureau unless before that time the request is approved, approved with a modification, or disapproved by the Secretary.

children.heading: Discontinuation of or decrease in rates
children.label: iv Discontinuation of or decrease in rates
I
children.text: the local school board requests that such differential be discontinued or decreased; or

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary or the supervisor, respectively, determines for clear and convincing reasons (and advises the board in writing of those reasons) that there is no disparity of compensation that would affect the recruitment or retention of employees at the school after the differential is discontinued or decreased.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: v Reports
children.text: On or before February 1 of each year, the Secretary shall submit to Congress a report describing the requests and approvals of authorization made under this paragraph during the previous year and listing the positions receiving post differential rates under contracts entered into under those authorizations.

children.heading: Liquidation of remaining leave upon termination
children.label: h Liquidation of remaining leave upon termination
children.text: Upon termination of employment with the Bureau, any annual leave remaining to the credit of an individual covered by this section shall be liquidated in accordance with sections 5551(a) and 6306 of title 5, except that leave earned or accrued under regulations promulgated pursuant to subsection (b)(10) shall not be so liquidated.

children.heading: Transfer of remaining sick leave upon transfer, promotion, or reemployment
children.label: i Transfer of remaining sick leave upon transfer, promotion, or reemployment
children.text: In the case of any educator who is transferred, promoted, or reappointed, without break in service, to a position in the Federal Government under a different leave system, any remaining leave to the credit of such person earned or credited under the regulations promulgated pursuant to subsection (b)(10) shall be transferred to such person’s credit in the employing agency on an adjusted basis in accordance with regulations which shall be promulgated by the Office of Personnel Management.

children.heading: Ineligibility for employment of voluntarily terminated educators
children.label: j Ineligibility for employment of voluntarily terminated educators
children.text: An educator who voluntarily terminates employment with the Bureau before the expiration of the existing employment contract between such educator and the Bureau shall not be eligible to be employed in another education position in the Bureau during the remainder of the term of such contract.
is employed at the close of a school year;
agrees in writing to serve in such position for the next school year; and

is employed in another position during the recess period immediately preceding such next school year, or during such recess period receives additional compensation referred to in , relating to dual compensation,section 5533 of title 5

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Secretary may, subject to the approval of the local school board concerned, accept voluntary services on behalf of Bureau schools.section 1342 of title 31

children.heading: Federal employee protection
children.label: 2 Federal employee protection
children.text: Nothing in this chapter requires Federal employees to work without compensation or allows the use of volunteer services to displace or replace Federal employees.

children.heading: Federal status
children.label: 3 Federal status
children.text: An individual providing volunteer services under this section is a Federal employee only for purposes of chapter 81 of title 5 and chapter 171 of title 28.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, including laws relating to dual compensation, the Secretary, at the election of the employee, shall prorate the salary of an employee employed in an education position for the academic school year over the entire 12-month period.

children.heading: Election
children.label: B Election
children.text: Each educator employed for the academic school year shall annually elect to be paid on a 12-month basis or for those months while school is in session.

children.heading: No loss of pay or benefits
children.label: C No loss of pay or benefits
children.text: No educator shall suffer a loss of pay or benefits, including benefits under unemployment or other Federal or federally assisted programs, because of such election.

children.heading: Change of election
children.label: 2 Change of election
children.text: During the course of such year the employee may change election once.

children.heading: Lump sum payment
children.label: 3 Lump sum payment
children.text: That portion of the employee’s pay which would be paid between academic school years may be paid in a lump sum at the election of the employee.

children.heading: Nonapplicability
children.label: 4 Nonapplicability
children.text: This subsection applies to those individuals employed under the provisions of this section or title 5.

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: 5 Definitions
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the terms “educator” and “education position” have the meanings contained in paragraphs (1) and (2) of subsection ().o

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Secretary may provide, for each Bureau area, a stipend in lieu of overtime premium pay or compensatory time off.

children.heading: Provision to employees
children.label: B Provision to employees
children.text: Any employee of the Bureau who performs additional activities to provide services to students or otherwise support the school’s academic and social programs may elect to be compensated for all such work on the basis of the stipend.

children.heading: Nature of stipend
children.label: C Nature of stipend
children.text: Such stipend shall be paid as a supplement to the employee’s base pay.

children.heading: Election not to receive stipend
children.label: 2 Election not to receive stipend
children.text: If an employee elects not to be compensated through the stipend established by this subsection, the appropriate provisions of title 5 shall apply.

children.heading: Applicability of subsection
children.label: 3 Applicability of subsection
children.text: This subsection applies to all Bureau employees, regardless of whether the employee is employed under this section or title 5.

children.label: i
children.text: are performed on a school year basis principally in a Bureau school; and

children.label: I
children.text: classroom or other instruction or the supervision or direction of classroom or other instruction;

children.label: II
children.text: any activity (other than teaching) which requires academic credits in educational theory and practice equal to the academic credits in educational theory and practice required for a bachelor’s degree in education from an accredited institution of higher education;

children.label: III
children.text: any activity in or related to the field of education notwithstanding that academic credits in educational theory and practice are not a formal requirement for the conduct of such activity; or

children.label: IV
children.text: support services at, or associated with, the site of the school; or

children.label: B
children.text: are performed at the agency level of the Bureau and involve the implementation of education-related programs other than the position for agency superintendent for education.

children.heading: Educator
children.label: 2 Educator
children.text: The term “educator” means an individual whose services are required, or who is employed, in an education position.

children.heading: Covered individuals; election
children.label: p Covered individuals; election
children.text: This section shall apply with respect to any educator hired after (and to any educator who elected for coverage under that provision after ) and to the position in which such individual is employed. The enactment of this section shall not affect the continued employment of an individual employed on , in an education position, or such person’s right to receive the compensation attached to such position.November 1, 1979November 1, 1979October 31, 1979

children.label: A
children.text: the supervisor, with the approval of the local school board (or of the education line officer upon appeal under paragraph (2)), of the Bureau school at which such educator provides services determines that a longer period of furlough is necessary due to an insufficient amount of funds available for personnel compensation at such school, as determined under the financial plan process as determined under section 2009(b) of this title; and6So in original. Probably should be section “2010(b)”.6

children.label: i
children.text: such educators are needed to operate summer programs, attend summer training sessions, or participate in special activities including curriculum development committees; and

children.label: ii
children.text: such educators are selected based upon such educator’s qualifications after public notice of the minimum qualifications reasonably necessary and without discrimination as to supervisory, nonsupervisory, or other status of the educators who apply.

children.heading: Appeals
children.label: 2 Appeals
children.text: The supervisor of a Bureau school may appeal to the appropriate agency education line officer any refusal by the local school board to approve any determination of the supervisor that is described in paragraph (1)(A) by filing a written statement describing the determination and the reasons the supervisor believes such determination should be approved. A copy of such statement shall be submitted to the local school board and such board shall be afforded an opportunity to respond, in writing, to such appeal. After reviewing such written appeal and response, the education line officer may, for good cause, approve the determination of the supervisor. The educational line officer shall transmit the determination of such appeal in the form of a written opinion to such local school board and to the supervisor identifying the reasons for approving such determination.

children.heading: Stipends
children.label: r Stipends
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to provide annual stipends to teachers who become certified by the National Board of Professional Teaching Standards, the National Council on Teacher Quality, or other nationally recognized certification or credentialing organizations.
